title: commelina erecta subsp. livingstonii
intro: Commelina erecta subsp. livingstonii is a subspecies of the Dayflower plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the family Commelinaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to alleviate various health issues, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Commelina erecta subsp. livingstonii are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
